subject: Product Management
title: Segway
description: A quick, practical guide to Segway.

hook.text: Before launch, Steve Jobs said Segway would be bigger than the PC. Bezos called it 'stirring.' It sold just 30,000 units.

buildup.text: Dean Kamen spent $100 million developing the Segway in secret. Investors expected it to replace walking.

discovery.text: The Segway was an engineering marvel — self-balancing gyroscopes in a personal transporter. The technology was stunning.

twist.text: At $5,000, it was too expensive for consumers. Cities banned it from sidewalks. It solved no urgent problem.

climax.text: Segway found a niche — mall cops and tour groups — but never came close to transforming transportation as promised.

punchline.text: Brilliant engineering can't fix the absence of a real problem.

quiz.question: What was the fundamental flaw in the Segway's product strategy?
options: It solved no urgent problem and was too expensive for its limited utility
It was too slow to be useful
